lessons.lesson: PF1 is a CLOSED CHAMBER machine with sag control and pre-blow. PF2 is an OPEN FRAME machine (no chamber) where material sags freely - for BATH INDUSTRY ONLY (bathtubs, spas). NEVER confuse these.
lessons.trigger: PF1 vs PF2 comparison or PF2 capabilities
lessons.correct_action: PF1 = closed chamber, versatile, automation. PF2 = open frame, basic, bath only.
lessons.incorrect_action: Never say PF2 has automation, advanced controls, or customization.

lessons.lesson: When quoting a machine price, ALWAYS include detailed tech specs as bullet points. Machine proposals must be 1200+ words.
lessons.correct_action: Full specs: forming area, heater power, vacuum, features, applications, price with disclaimer.
lessons.incorrect_action: Never send a short price-only email without specs.

lessons.lesson: PF2 has NO automation options, NO chamber, NO sag control. Air cylinder driven. Bath industry ONLY. NEVER hallucinate PF2 features.
lessons.correct_action: PF2 = basic, air cylinder, open frame, bath industry only.
lessons.incorrect_action: NEVER claim PF2 has mold design customization, heating config, advanced controls, material compatibility, or automation.

lessons.lesson: Ensure to acknowledge and utilize information already provided by the customer to avoid redundancy.
lessons.trigger: When a customer repeats their requirements or constraints clearly.
lessons.correct_action: Acknowledge the information and provide a solution or alternative based on the given details.
lessons.incorrect_action: Asking the same questions repeatedly without progressing the conversation.

lessons.lesson: Ensure all technical details, especially regarding energy consumption and integration timelines, are accurate and clearly explained.
lessons.trigger: When discussing technical specifications or integration processes
lessons.correct_action: Provide accurate data and, if possible, examples or case studies to support claims.
lessons.incorrect_action: Avoid providing vague or incorrect technical details that could mislead the customer.

lessons.lesson: Provide specific information and address customer queries directly.
lessons.trigger: When a customer asks for detailed specifications or has specific concerns.
lessons.correct_action: Provide detailed answers and address specific customer queries.
lessons.incorrect_action: Avoid repeating questions without adding value or addressing the customer's concerns.

lessons.lesson: Provide specific examples or data when discussing technical trade-offs.
lessons.trigger: When a customer asks about the impact of removing or altering features.
lessons.correct_action: Provide data or case studies that illustrate the impact of changes.
lessons.incorrect_action: Avoid vague responses without supporting data.

calliope.feedback: Calliope should learn to better utilize customer-provided information to avoid redundancy and improve the flow of the conversation.
Calliope should focus on improving the accuracy of technical details and ensure that all customer queries are addressed with specific examples or case studies when requested.
Calliope should learn to provide more detailed responses to customer inquiries, especially regarding technical specifications and maintenance.
Calliope should learn to avoid repeating qualification questions and focus on providing specific technical insights when discussing feature trade-offs.
